Commercial Window Cleaning — What's Included

Professional window cleaning for storefronts, offices, and commercial buildings. Regular service contracts keep your business looking professional year-round.

  • Storefront glass — streak-free, ready for business
  • Office building windows — interior and exterior
  • Recurring weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly contracts
  • After-hours scheduling available on request
  • Commercial invoice and receipt provided

After a commercial clean, storefront glass is clear and reflective — product displays are visible from the street, interior lighting isn't filtered through grime, and the overall professional impression of the business is measurably better. Regular clients consistently report that clean windows are one of the most commented-on maintenance details by their own customers, even when those customers can't articulate exactly what changed.

Our Commercial Window Cleaning Process

Every job follows the same proven process — no shortcuts, no variation by location.

  1. 1. Site Assessment & Scheduling Agreement

    Before the first clean, the site is assessed for window count, access points, height requirements, and any security or safety considerations. For commercial properties, scheduling is critical — the clean needs to happen during low-traffic hours to avoid disrupting customers or staff. The assessment results in a written scope and agreed service schedule, not an open-ended arrangement.

  2. 2. Low-Traffic Window Scheduling

    Commercial cleans are scheduled for early morning, evening, or mid-week off-peak slots depending on the business type. A restaurant front cleaned during the lunch rush is an interruption; cleaned at 7am, it's invisible maintenance. The service schedule is confirmed with the business manager and held consistently so staff don't need to coordinate each visit.

  3. 3. Debris & Frame Pre-Clean

    Frames, sills, and door surrounds are pre-cleaned of debris, insect residue, and grime before solution is applied to the glass. On storefronts, this includes the door frame channel and any transom ledges where debris accumulates unnoticed. Pre-cleaning the frame is essential on commercial glass — solution applied over a dirty frame picks up contaminants that then streak across the freshly cleaned pane.

  4. 4. Professional Solution Application

    Commercial-grade window cleaning solution is applied with a wide-format applicator suited to large storefront panes. The solution is worked in firm, even strokes to ensure complete coverage without dry patches. Concentration is adjusted based on soiling level — a downtown Salmon Arm storefront near a busy street accumulates vehicle exhaust residue faster than a rural office building and may require a stronger mix.

  5. 5. Squeegee & Edge Detail

    Each pane is worked with a squeegee sized for the glass — large pulls on wide storefront windows, smaller tools for divided-light sections. Every edge and corner is hand-detailed with a lint-free cloth immediately after the squeegee pass. Detail work on commercial glass matters more than residential because customers and passersby view the glass at close range and at eye level.

  6. 6. Door Glass & Entry Cleaning

    Entry doors and glass door panels are cleaned last, after foot traffic during setup is no longer a factor. Door glass accumulates fingerprints, nose smudges, and push-bar grease that require specific attention — a squeegee alone does not fully remove body oils from glass. Each door pane is wiped, squeegeed, and re-inspected before moving on.

  7. 7. Manager Walkthrough & Sign-Off

    At the end of each visit, the result is reviewed with the business owner or manager on-site — or photographed and sent if they're not present. This creates accountability and catches any spots that need immediate correction. For recurring contract clients, a brief note on any developing issues — frame deterioration, seal failure, staining — is included so the business can act before problems escalate.

What makes us different: Wagon Windows offers commercial clients in the Shuswap something chains and franchises can't — a named, local operator who shows up consistently, knows your property, and communicates directly. No dispatched crews, no turnover, no having to re-explain the job every visit.

Common Problems We Solve

  • Downtown Salmon Arm storefronts accumulate vehicle exhaust film that makes glass appear cloudy from inside and dirty from outside within weeks of a clean
  • Agricultural dust from the surrounding Salmon Arm farming corridor coats commercial windows heavily in dry summer months, requiring more frequent service than urban businesses expect
  • Cottonwood season creates a visible pollen film on storefront glass that customers notice and that cannot be casually wiped — it requires proper washing to remove cleanly
  • Lake tourism season brings higher foot traffic that leaves fingerprints, smudges, and sunscreen residue on entry glass at a rate that monthly service can't keep up with
  • Wildfire smoke periods deposit oily residue on exterior commercial glass that standard cleaning products streak rather than remove, requiring professional-grade emulsifiers

Pricing for Commercial Window Cleaning

Commercial storefronts: From $119 per visit. Recurring contracts available.

Frequently Asked Questions — Commercial Window Cleaning

Do you offer recurring commercial window cleaning contracts?

Yes — we offer weekly, bi-weekly, and monthly recurring contracts for storefronts, offices, and commercial buildings. Recurring contracts get priority scheduling and consistent pricing.

Can you clean commercial windows outside business hours?

We can arrange early morning or weekend scheduling for businesses that need cleaning done before opening. Contact us to discuss your schedule.

Do you provide invoices for commercial window cleaning?

Yes — we provide commercial invoices and receipts for every job. GST/HST documentation available on request.

What areas does Wagon Windows serve?

Wagon Windows serves the entire Shuswap region from our Salmon Arm base: Salmon Arm, Sorrento, Tappen, Blind Bay, Sicamous, Chase, Enderby, and Armstrong. Contact us if your area isn't listed — we travel for the right job.

How do I get a quote from Wagon Windows?

The fastest way is to book a free 30-minute consultation via our Calendly link at wagonwindows.com — you'll have a confirmed appointment within minutes. You can also call or text (403) 837-5092, or email davidhanna@wagonwindow.com. We respond within a few hours.

Is Wagon Windows insured?

Yes — Wagon Windows carries liability insurance. We treat your property with care and take full responsibility for our work on every job.

What is the minimum job fee?

Our minimum job fee is $99. This covers smaller jobs, single-room requests, and add-ons like screen cleaning or track cleaning on their own.
